title: rubus menkei subsp. henriquesii
intro: Rubus menkei subsp. henriquesii, commonly known as the Madeira raspberry, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. It is a type of bramble that belongs to the Rubus genus and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid. The Madeira raspberry has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and cardiovascular diseases, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus menkei subsp. henriquesii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are also used in the production of jams, jellies, and other food products.
